project_name: Capability Retention Sentinel Live Baseline Adapter Run

decision.recommended_next_action: Promote the live-baseline adapter artifacts and use `run-openai-compatible` plus `compare-live-baseline` against any controller-selected real baseline/compressed endpoint model pair.
decision.stop_reason: The branch now has executable parent suite artifacts, a verified OpenAI-compatible live adapter, named-model baseline/compressed comparison artifacts, unit coverage, and a real llama.cpp endpoint smoke with no helper servers left running.

recommended_next_action: Promote the live-baseline adapter artifacts and use `run-openai-compatible` plus `compare-live-baseline` against any controller-selected real baseline/compressed endpoint model pair.
run_notes_tail: - The suite now has a reusable model-output adapter in addition to deterministic generation/scoring and closeout JSON reporting.
- The core hypothesis is supported for an MVP: the sentinel suite catches intentional regressions across all required categories and can be wired to local model runners without adding dependencies.
- Recommended next action is to finalize this project positive and queue a separate downstream project only if a controller wants live baseline measurements on specific local models.
- Copied parent suite source, tests, prompts, reports, and README into this branch to avoid a routing-only successor.
- Added `sentinel_suite/live_baseline.py` with OpenAI-compatible endpoint contract verification, `/chat/completions` prompt execution, report scoring helper, and baseline-vs-compressed comparison.
- Added `scripts/mock_openai_server.py`, an OpenAI-compatible local mock with two named models: `local-baseline-mock` and `local-compressed-mock`.
- Added unit coverage for the live adapter using a local HTTP server.
- `python -m unittest discover -s tests -v` passed: 6 tests OK.
- `reports/live/mock_comparison.json`: retention gate fail, delta -1.0 across all categories.
- Ran 3 exact-copy prompts; runner failures 0, but scored 0/3 due nonsensical model outputs.

- The branch hypothesis is supported for the adapter/integration mechanism: this project now has executable branch artifacts, named endpoint model verification, live response capture, deterministic scoring, and baseline/candidate comparison.
- The Phi-4 Q4 local smoke is not evidence of capability retention; it is endpoint/adapter evidence plus a negative model-output smoke result on 3 exact-copy cases.
- No helper servers were left running.
- The suite now has a reusable model-output adapter in addition to deterministic generation/scoring and closeout JSON reporting.
- The core hypothesis is supported for an MVP: the sentinel suite catches intentional regressions across all required categories and can be wired to local model runners without adding dependencies.
- Recommended next action is to finalize this project positive and queue a separate downstream project only if a controller wants live baseline measurements on specific local models.
